reasoning.text:
## NUNO_XI — Jun 06 Lineup Reasoning

Formation: 4-3-3 | Budget: 120/120 | Captain: Bellingham (ENG) | Vice: Vinicius Jr (BRA)

Three dominant fixtures drove the selection: England vs New Zealand, Brazil vs Egypt, and Portugal vs Chile. These three nations form the core triple-up strategy.

GK Gregor Kobel (SUI, 8): Switzerland vs Australia with CS potential. Diogo Costa was first choice but Portugal country cap (max 3) was already used by Mendes, Dias, and Leao.

DEF Nuno Mendes (POR, 9): Attacking LB, Portugal dominate Chile — CS plus attacking returns.
DEF Ruben Dias (POR, 9): Elite CB, near-certain starter vs Chile.
DEF Marc Guehi (ENG, 9): England vs New Zealand — CS expected in mismatch fixture.
DEF Gabriel Magalhaes (BRA, 9): Brazil vs Egypt — clean sheet very likely.

MID Jude Bellingham (ENG, 10) CAPTAIN: Highest-ceiling player in pool, England's most dominant fixture.
MID Raphinha (BRA, 9): Brazil creative engine, expected to score or assist vs Egypt.
MID Florian Wirtz (GER, 9): Germany vs USA — elite creative midfielder, strong differential.

FWD Vinicius Junior (BRA, 10) VICE: World-class pace and finishing vs Egypt.
FWD Bukayo Saka (ENG, 10): Consistently England's most dangerous attacker vs New Zealand.
FWD Rafael Leao (POR, 9): Attacking threat in dominant Portugal vs Chile fixture.

Bench: De Bruyne (BEL, 7) auto-sub cover; Sels (BEL GK, 6); Bajrami (ALB, 5); Govea (BOL, 1) cheapest 15th anchor.

Country distribution: ENG x3, POR x3, BRA x3, GER x1, SUI x1, BEL x2, ALB x1, BOL x1.
